task_completion.description: Load the topographic survey dataset 'local_datum_survey.csv' into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An import dialog showing the selection of 'local_datum_survey.csv' from 'C:\workspace\data\', or the TopoCal workspace displaying the loaded survey points.

task_completion.description: Access the tool or menu in TopoCal to adjust the elevation or shift the coordinates of the points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu, ribbon button, or dialog box related to moving, shifting, or adjusting elevations/Z-coordinates is open and active.

task_completion.description: Input the exact Z-shift value of +845.32 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text field or input box within the TopoCal adjustment tool showing the exact value '845.32' for the Z-axis or elevation shift.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export of the adjusted point list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export menu, point export tool, or 'Save As' dialog specifically configured for exporting point data to a CSV format.

task_completion.description: Save the exported point list as 'navd88_survey.csv' in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog showing the file name 'navd88_survey.csv' and the destination path 'C:\workspace\data\'.
A file save dialog for the project showing 'datum_shift.top' in 'C:\workspace\data\', or the TopoCal application title bar displaying 'datum_shift.top' after saving.

integrity.description: The agent used the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform the adjustments and exports.
integrity.visual_evidence: TopoCal application windows, menus, and dialogs are visible in the screenshots, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts manipulating the CSV directly.

integrity.description: The Z-shift value was entered into the actual TopoCal application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value '845.32' is seen inside a TopoCal dialog box or tool panel, not just typed into a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent exported the new CSV and saved the project from within TopoCal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Export and Save dialogs originate from the TopoCal application, proving the files were generated by the software rather than manually created or renamed in the file explorer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input dataset: 'C:\workspace\data\local_datum_survey.csv'. Expected Z-shift: +845.32 meters (tolerance 0.05m). Expected exported CSV: 'C:\workspace\data\navd88_survey.csv'. Expected saved TopoCal project: 'C:\workspace\data\datum_shift.top'.
